title: Release Notes & Frequently Asked Questions | Respiratory Illnesses | CDC
text:
dicaid Services (CMS) requires acute care and critical access hospitals to electronically report information via NHSN regarding COVID-19, influenza, RSV hospitalizations, and hospital bed capacity and occupancy data to CDC's National Healthcare Safety Network. For more information about reporting to NHSN, visit Hospital Respiratory Data | NHSN | CDC . 
Why: 
This addition aims to enhance situational awareness by providing near-real-time insights into hospitalization trends associated with respiratory illnesses for both the nation and states/territories. 
2. Updated the data notes for the Respiratory Virus Activity Level page
What: 
We added more details to the methods used for the Acute Respiratory Index baseline metric for measuring the level of respiratory illness activity. 
Why: 
These changes will help others understand the methods and allow them to replicate the approach. 
3. Design updates for greater usability in November & December 2024
Update #1: 
What: We added an activity level legend to the What To Know section of the Respiratory Illnesses Data Channel . 
Why: A legend clarifies the colors used to signify activity levels and puts the current level into context for users. 
Update #2: 
What: We changed the placement of y-axis labels on charts, so they appear at the top of the axis instead of along the left side.
